overview.description: The iShares High Yield Systematic Bond ETF (HYDB) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in high yield fixed income. The fund tracks a broad-maturity, multifactor index of high-yield bonds. The index selects and weights bonds based on default probability, default-adjusted spreads, and volatility. HYDB was launched on Jul 11, 2017 and is issued by BlackRock.
infoTable: BlackRock High Yield Systematic Bond Index

data.title: High-yield, low visibility: Strategies for trading bond ETFs as rates rise
data.text: Stephen Laipply of BlackRock iShares, Isaac Braley of BTS Asset Management and John Davi of Astoria Portfolio Advisors talk their strategies for high-yield investments as interest rates rise. With CNB...

data.title: High-yield bond funds still look attractive, says Stephen BlackRock's Laipply
data.text: CNBC's Bob Pisani discusses managing high yield bond ETFs as interest rates rise with Steve Laipply of BlackRock iShares and Isaac Braley of BTS Management.
